community.lede: AGPL-3.0 on GitHub. Issues and the roadmap live there; if you want to see what's coming next or what broke last week, it's all on the commit log.
community.hero_title: Star the repo, shape the roadmap.
community.hero_desc: Every issue, PR and roadmap item is public on GitHub. เปิด an issue, comment on what matters, or send a PR — contributions of any size are welcome.
community.modcard_1_desc: Getting started, module SDK, API reference and deployment guides.
community.modcard_2_desc: Every bug, feature request and roadmap item is tracked publicly.
community.modcard_3_desc: Cookiecutter template, typed SDK, OpenAPI auto-gen. Ship a plugin in an afternoon.
community.modcard_4_desc: Questions, ideas and show-and-tell. Hop in for things that aren’t quite an issue yet.

tour.lede: Seven steps from first-run to finished tender. Follow them in order or jump ahead — each one plays a short capture from the running app.
tour.info_desc_default: First run. Pick language, region and currency — the app comes with a starter ฐานข้อมูลต้นทุน (55,000+ priced positions) already loaded. Zero setup, no seed-data dance.
tour.chip2_title: สร้าง a โครงการ

steps.caption: 01 · onboarding
steps.title: Onboarding
steps.desc: First run. Pick language, region and currency — the app comes with a starter ฐานข้อมูลต้นทุน (55,000+ priced positions) already loaded. Zero setup, no seed-data dance.

steps.caption: 02 · create a โครงการ
steps.title: สร้าง a โครงการ
steps.desc: Name it, set the location, invite the team. Every โครงการ gets its own dashboard, document room, กฎการตรวจสอบ and a set of estimates. Switch between projects in one click.

steps.caption: 03 · start the estimate
steps.title: Start the estimate
steps.desc: เปิด a new BOQ. Pick a classification tree — DIN 276, NRM, MasterFormat, or your own — and start building sections. Drag to reorder, nest any depth, copy from templates.

steps.caption: 04 · add positions
steps.title: Add positions
steps.desc: ค้นหา 55,000+ priced positions by trade, region or keyword. Drop them straight into the BOQ with their หน่วย rates already filled in. Typeahead, semantic search, instant totals.

steps.caption: 05 · resources & assemblies
steps.title: Resources & assemblies
steps.desc: Each รายการ holds its resource breakdown — materials, labour, อุปกรณ์ — grouped as assemblies. Override rates, apply regional factors, watch totals recalculate across the โครงการ.

steps.caption: 06 · link BIM
steps.title: Link the BIM model
steps.desc: Drop an IFC or RVT file. Select walls, slabs, openings — push quantities straight into the BOQ. Re-link on model revision; the positions keep their rates and comments.

steps.caption: 07 · link CAD / PDF
steps.title: Link CAD & PDF drawings
steps.desc: DWG and DGN with layer-aware selection. PDF with click-to-measure and AI pre-fill. Bulk-link groups — dozens of elements into positions in one pass.

steps.caption: 08 · ตรวจสอบ
steps.title: ตรวจสอบ the estimate
steps.desc: Traffic-light pass across the whole BOQ. Built-in rule sets for DIN 276, NRM, MasterFormat and GAEB. Plus quality rules — zero prices, missing quantities, outlier rates — flagged inline.

steps.caption: 09 · tasks & follow-ups
steps.title: งาน & follow-ups
steps.desc: Every flagged row, BIM element or reviewer comment can become a task. Kanban board per โครงการ, assignees, due dates, status columns — the estimators' to-do list lives where the estimate lives.
chips: ความคิดเห็น

steps.caption: 10 · ส่งออก the tender
steps.title: ส่งออก the tender
steps.desc: Totals roll up per section, trade and discipline. ส่งออก GAEB X83/X84/X89, Excel, PDF or JSON. Send the tender pack to subcontractors, collect bids, compare side-by-side.

compare.lede: Honest table. Commercial suites are genuinely better at a few things — mostly integrations paid for over a decade. Everywhere else, the gap is smaller than sales teams claim.
compare.row_55k: 55,000+ cost items out of the box
compare.note: ✓ fully supported  ·  ~ partial or via plugin  ·  ✗ not supported

module_lab.eyebrow: modular by ออกแบบ
module_lab.title:
Add a module. Remove one.
ไม่ rebuild.
module_lab.sub: Every feature is a self-contained package. Drop it in, pull it out — the running install picks it up in seconds, zero downtime.

developers.lede: Every feature is a Python package with a manifest. You can add one to a running install without rebuilding anything. The APIs are typed; the OpenAPI spec is generated, not hand-maintained.
developers.bullet1_text: Module SDK — scaffold one with make module-new
developers.bullet2: <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><polyline points="20 6 9 17 4 12"/></svg>Typed REST API, auto-generated OpenAPI spec
developers.bullet3: <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><polyline points="20 6 9 17 4 12"/></svg>Hooks & event bus — extend without forking

voices.para1: Over the past ten years, I have been deeply involved in resource management for construction projects. This journey inevitably led me to study the history of the technologies that have shaped the industry—from the earliest attempts at ออกแบบ automation to modern ERP platforms (see the series of articles “The Lobbyists’ Wars and the Development of BIM” and “The History of the BIM Map”). Without understanding where we came from, it is impossible to see where we are going.
voices.para2: Over the years, dozens of articles have come off my desk, read by millions of professionals around the world. At the same time, I’ve consulted with major construction and consulting firms, developers, and software vendors themselves on data management in projects—helping them navigate processes where data is not a byproduct but the foundation for decision-making. This work gave me a rare opportunity to see the industry from both sides: through the eyes of those who create the tools and through the eyes of those who use them in real projects every day. Many of these observations and reflections are collected in my book Data-Driven Construction, which is now available in 16 languages—datadrivenconstruction.io/books.
voices.para3: Two other things have kept me busy: helping non-developers get into proprietary formats that were never meant to be opened, and finding a cleaner way to describe construction work through a resource model. These efforts have resulted in free tools—DDC CAD/BIM data converters (Revit, IFC, DWG, DGN → structured data), available on GitHub, and the multilingual CWICR database of construction works and resources—over 55,000 items in 11 languages, published as OpenConstructionEstimate-DDC-CWICR. All of this was a necessary step toward an idea I’ve been pursuing for the past decade — an open-source modular ERP for the construction industry.
voices.changelog_more: Full changelog on GitHub <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="12" height="12" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M7 7h10v10"/><path d="M7 17 17 7"/></svg>
voices.para4: The recent generation of AI tooling finally made it feasible to consolidate that work — methodology, data models, and prior implementations — into a single platform. It’s now public and open source.
voices.pull: Progress is born from dialogue—from the clash of perspectives and openness to new approaches. I would be grateful if you would be willing to participate in this conversation on the inevitable Uberization of the construction industry and the transparency of cost and time estimation processes for construction projects.

pricing.lede: Two paths. Run it yourself for free under AGPL-3.0, or talk to us when you need hosted support, a commercial license for the converters, or anything else that shouldn't live in the open-source license.

license.lede: The core is AGPL-3.0 — great for self-hosting, not always a fit for proprietary or embedded products. A commercial license lets you ship OpenConstructionERP inside a closed-source product, on customer infrastructure or as part of a paid SaaS — with a signed agreement, invoicing and support.
license.reassure2: OEM, embedding and closed-source use covered
license.reassure3: SLA, priority support and success manager
license.use_internal: ภายใน use only (AGPL may be fine)
license.use_embedded: Embedded in our own commercial software
license.field_message: Anything you'd like us to know
license.field_message_placeholder: Deployment scale, timeline, specific compliance needs…
license.consent: I agree that OpenConstructionERP / DataDrivenConstruction may contact me about this inquiry, store the submitted data for the purpose of responding, and use my email to send occasional product news and updates. You can unsubscribe at any time.

faq.q1: Is the core really free and open-source?
faq.a1: ใช่. The core is AGPL-3.0 on GitHub. You can self-host forever, audit every line, fork it, or run it in production. We also offer a commercial license for enterprises that can't use AGPL.
faq.q2: What hardware do I need to run it?
faq.a2: The core runs on a 2 GB VPS. One PostgreSQL instance is all you need for a small team. AI features (takeoff, vector search) are optional — features degrade gracefully when AI services aren't available.
faq.q3: How do I นำเข้า from my existing system?
faq.a3: Native GAEB XML (X83 / X84 / X89), Excel and CSV นำเข้า are built in. Connectors for proprietary systems ship as modules — the marketplace already has a handful, including workflow integration via n8n. Custom migrations are available via Enterprise support.
faq.q4: Do I need AI services to use the product?
faq.a4: ไม่. The BOQ editor, การตรวจสอบ pipeline, CAD ingest and ฐานข้อมูลต้นทุน all work without AI. AI takeoff and semantic search are optional enhancements — you can bring your own model or use the hosted CV/LLM services via Pro.
faq.a5: Built by DataDrivenConstruction — the team behind CWICR (the 55,000-item ฐานข้อมูลต้นทุน shipped here), cad2data, and 10+ years of construction-tech engineering. We ship our own tools on it.
faq.a6: Absolutely. The SDK ships with a cookiecutter template — scaffold a module in one command, drop it in the marketplace when it's ready. Good candidates: regional cost databases, ERP connectors, custom การตรวจสอบ rule sets.
faq.q7: Where is my data stored? Does anything leave my server?
faq.a7: On your infrastructure. โครงการ, drawings and cost data live in your PostgreSQL and your object storage — nothing is phoned home, no telemetry by default. If you enable hosted AI services, only the payload you send is processed; it's opt-in per workspace and you can point the client at self-hosted models instead.
faq.q8: What CAD and BIM formats are supported?
faq.a8: DWG, DGN, RVT and IFC ingest through the cad2data pipeline into a canonical format — one schema to query, classify and cost against. PDF drawings go through the takeoff pipeline (vector extraction + OCR). New formats land as modules, not core rewrites.
faq.q9: How does the commercial license work?
faq.a9: AGPL-3.0 is fine for most internal and service use. If you embed the product in a closed SaaS, ship it inside a proprietary application, or need an explicit waiver for procurement, talk to us — commercial terms cover that, plus priority support and enterprise SLA. ไม่ per-seat metering, no forced cloud.
faq.a10: ใช่, and we mean it. Everything you put in comes back out — GAEB XML, Excel, CSV, JSON, Parquet, or a direct PostgreSQL dump. The schema is documented, the source is open; no proprietary file formats, no escrow games, no hostage data.
faq.q11: How often do you ship updates?
faq.a11: Minor releases roughly every 2–3 weeks, patch releases as needed. Changelog lives on GitHub, every tagged version has a Docker image, and migrations are reversible. You decide when to upgrade — self-hosted means nobody forces a deploy window on you.
faq.q12: Which languages and regions are supported?
faq.a12: 30 languages ship in the core i18n. ฐานข้อมูลต้นทุน labels, การตรวจสอบ rule sets and classification trees are per-region modules — pick the ones you need, add your own. ไม่ hard-coded currency, no hard-coded date format.
